_comment: Tracks versioned frameworks whose major/minor changes may invalidate seeds. Seeds for these stacks gain a reviewed_at field. Seeds that have not been reviewed since a version bump are flagged as stale candidates by flag-stale-seeds.mjs.
_volatile_criteria: A stack is volatile when its API, patterns, or best-practices change materially between major or minor versions — not when patches are released.

react.note: Hooks, concurrent features, StrictMode behaviour, server components

next.note: App Router, RSC, routing, data fetching patterns change across minor versions

angular.note: Standalone components, signals, control flow syntax

svelte.note: Svelte 5 runes represent a major paradigm shift

kubernetes.note: API deprecations, resource versions, admission webhooks

terraform.note: Provider protocol, state format, provider configuration changes

typescript.note: Type inference improvements, decorator changes, module resolution

nodejs.note: ESM interop, fetch, Web Streams, permissions model

rust.note: Rust is very stable — edition-level changes only, and those are explicit

go.note: Go compatibility guarantee means patterns rarely invalidate

python.note: Language-level — seeds about stdlib specifics may need minor review

universal.note: Cross-cutting concepts — no version to track

machine-learning.note: Statistical concepts are stable; specific library seeds (pytorch, sklearn) tracked separately
